question: The scaffold judge is a keyed real-provider model (google/gemini-1.5-pro-002), so a keyless first-timer gets every comparison ledgered as CANT_JUDGE(provider_error) [F4]. Ship the keyless deterministic judge (fake/deterministic-2026-01-01) in the single-source starter template with an adjacent comment showing the real-provider swap line, or keep the real judge and document the key requirement?
author: claude/design

question: When every grade or judgment lands cant_* (a fail-closed outcome), should the bench verb exit non-zero to signal 'nothing scored', or stay 0 and move disclosure into the summary line?
author: claude/design

question: judge.panel is schema-accepted but read by nothing [F9]: setting it silently changes the spec hash and does nothing else. Remove the field entirely, or keep it in the schema as a v2 breadcrumb but refuse with a typed error when set?
author: claude/design

question: A task suite with fewer than two tasks can never yield a paired decision (the bootstrap clusters on tasks) [F3/F-H7], disclosed only at analyze time today. Should plan gate (refuse to lock), warn (lock but name the consequence), or stay silent?
author: claude/design

question: A fixture-id judgment call surfaced at Batch A review: tests/fixtures/builders.py carries a hardcoded 'exp-fixture' default experiment_id. Keep the invisible default, or eliminate it suite-wide and shift fixtures to the dir-derived pattern (real-dir fixtures derive the id through the AC-1 identity seam; dir-less unit tests pass an explicit literal at the call site)?
author: claude/review

question: AC-1 as drafted scoped the experiment_id resolved-path fix to plan only, but F1's defect is 'every event': bench run . and bench grade . still ledger experiment_id='' on trial/grade events. Broaden AC-1 to every derivation site through one shared seam, or keep the plan-only scope and defer the stage events?
author: claude/review

question: AC-3's draft summary named only the dominant cant_judge reason. Keep the single dominant reason, or emit per-reason counts (the full reason map riding the outcome), matching grade's AC-2 line?
author: claude/review

question: Flipping the starter template to contender-first plus fake judge (AC-7) ripples into tests whose expectations froze under the old control-first/google-judge defaults. Flip every dependent fixture's literals to track the new default, or pin each test's historical inputs explicitly so frozen goldens stay byte-untouched?
author: claude/review

rationale: Option A ruled 2026-07-07 during the live first-run walkthrough: the starter template ships fake/deterministic-2026-01-01 with an adjacent comment showing the real-provider swap line, so the default path reaches a decision keyless. The usage-guide section 2.1 example keeps the real Gemini judge; the single-source test compares field sets, not values.
migration: template-only; the scaffold's judge id changes, no locked spec or hash chain is touched.
author: jyang

rationale: Ruled 2026-07-07: a fail-closed outcome is a completed, ledgered operation, not a command failure - the command did its job and recorded the honest result. Exit stays 0; the scored/cant_* split is disclosed in the AC-2/AC-3 summary lines.
migration: none - behavior-only; exit codes and ledger events unchanged.
author: jyang

rationale: Ruled 2026-07-07: judge.panel stays in the schema as the v2 breadcrumb but refuses when set, with a typed SpecError naming the field and the fix - closing the silent no-op the extra='forbid' posture exists to prevent, at no capability cost today (nothing sets it; the one serializer fixture is covered by the test-change register).
migration: additive validation only; default None unchanged and every existing fixture spec validates unchanged; a spec that set panel (never a green path) now refuses at load.
author: jyang

rationale: Ruled 2026-07-07: plan warns but never gates - a stdout line naming the consequence plus an additive 'insufficient_tasks_for_decision' flag on the lock event. Single-task exploratory designs stay legitimate and lockable; the disclosure moves to the moment the design is created, not the end of the pipeline.
migration: additive flag string in the lock event's existing flags vector; no schema or hash-chain change; two-or-more-task plans carry neither warning nor flag.
author: jyang

rationale: Ruled by the human at Batch A review 2026-07-07: eliminate the hardcoded 'exp-fixture' default entirely; fixtures with a real experiment dir derive their id through the harness.ledger identity seam AC-1 introduces, and dir-less unit tests pass an explicit literal at the call site - never an invisible default. Test-infrastructure only; the enforceable production behavior is AC-1's.
migration: test-infrastructure only; asserted id literals update mechanically ('exp-fixture' -> dir name or explicit literal); no production contract touched.
author: jyang

rationale: Resolved at Batch A review 2026-07-07 by the orchestrator under jyang's standing auto-accept mandate ('accept strong recommendations automatically'): the plan-only scope under-delivered F1's 'every event' - run and grade still baked '' into the permanent chain. AC-1 is broadened so plan, run, grade, and cli_common.event_context all derive the id from the RESOLVED directory name through one shared harness.ledger seam.
migration: none - the id was already directory-derived; only the resolution site changes, correcting the '' the unresolved path baked in.
author: claude/orchestrator (jyang auto-accept mandate)

rationale: Resolved at Batch B review 2026-07-07 by the orchestrator under jyang's standing auto-accept mandate: per-reason counts replace the draft's 'dominant reason' - strictly more informative, shape-consistent with grade's AC-2 line, and the full reason map rides the outcome. Also registered the mechanical e2e literal update (container_failure -> holdout_results_missing on the local missing-results path) under the test-change register's sweep-and-list rule.
migration: none - summary-line content only; JudgeOutcome carries an additive cant_judge_reasons map.
author: claude/orchestrator (jyang auto-accept mandate)

rationale: Resolved at Batch D review 2026-07-07 by the orchestrator under jyang's standing auto-accept mandate (the implementation agent had escalated a golden re-freeze; the ruling dissolved it): two sanctioned shapes, chosen per test for transparency - (a) tests OF template-derived flows flip their arm-order/delta-sign/judge-id literals to track the new default; (b) tests indifferent to arm order (the frozen forensics_report seam golden, schedule-dependent trajectory/kappa/detector fixtures) pin their historical inputs explicitly so frozen expectations, including the seam golden's EXPECTED_REPORT, stay byte-untouched. No golden or snapshot literal changes; no assertion deleted or weakened. Same explicit-inputs-over-invisible-defaults principle as D5.
migration: test-fixture inputs pinned explicitly; no golden or snapshot literal changed; no production contract touched.
author: claude/orchestrator (jyang auto-accept mandate)
